title: Ixora borneensis
taxonomy: Ixora borneensis is classified within the family Rubiaceae. This family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. intro: Ixora borneensis, a species of flowering plant in the Rubiaceae family, is native to Borneo and is commonly known as the Bornean Ixora or Jungle Geranium. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Ixora borneensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant are teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used in traditional medicine to alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
